original_reasoning: Arabic خلط includes mental corruption or confusion, and χολωθείς means angered; both sides meet in disturbed inner state, with action drifting into emotional result.
Being loud voiced or becoming well known gives a plausible speech-centered bridge to the language or dialect marker.
Arabic gloss names a plant type, Greek gloss names an eye-salve; a plausible medicinal-botanical chain exists.
Arabic غاية is limit/end-point, and a spear-head is the pointed terminal part of the weapon.
Arabic = abundant trees/palms; Greek = Gaetuli (North African tribe). Phonetic match g-t-l and Gaetuli were North African which fits geographic overlap; plausible substrate echo.
نكحها carries taking/receiving a mate, a plausible but not direct link to receive.
Arabic includes exposed flesh/fat appearing outward; Greek means to gape or open wide. The shared notion of opening/exposure is plausible.
The Arabic gloss explicitly includes a place-name, and the Greek lemma is also a place-name, giving a plausible toponymic chain.
